_description: Per-issuer extraction hints for the PRISM extractor. Each issuer entry contains section headings, field aliases (PRISM dot-path → list of synonyms found in that issuer's filings), and notes. Grounded in actual filing text from data/filings/ folders.
_usage: Prepend relevant issuer hints to the extraction prompt when issuer_name matches. Apply field_level_hints universally regardless of issuer.

section_headings: Key Terms Relating to the Review Dates and Interest Payment Dates
Payments in Connection with the First Review Date
Payments in Connection with Review Dates (Other than the First and Final Review Dates)
JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC.key_terms_position: early — 'Key Terms' section appears within the first 200 lines of the pricing supplement, formatted as a two-column table with brown/gold headers (#54301A color in HTML)
JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC.document_structure: Two-page cover format: page 1 is the front cover with product name and key economic terms summary; page 2 is 'Key Terms' table with full field details

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.format: Full index name, e.g. 'The MerQube US Small-Cap Vol Advantage Index'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.typical_location: Key Terms table, first data row

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].bloo.format: Bloomberg ticker in parentheses after index name, e.g. '(Bloomberg ticker: MQUSSVA)'

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.format: Absolute index level, e.g. '747.51' or 'The closing level of the Index on the Pricing Date, which was 747.51'

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.format: 'The closing level of the Index on the final Review Date'

coupon.rate.format: Percentage per annum with quarterly payment split, e.g. '13.75% per annum, payable at a rate of 3.4375% per quarter'. Dollar amount also given: '$34.375 per $1,000'

coupon.barrierLevel.format: Percentage of Initial Value as both percentage and absolute level: '60.00% of the Initial Value, which is 448.506'
coupon.barrierLevel._note: JPMorgan often combines Interest Barrier and Trigger Value into a single field. When they are the same value, both the coupon barrier and barrier trigger level are identical.

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.format: Percentage of Initial Value: '60.00% of the Initial Value, which is 448.506'. Extract the percentage (0.60).

autocall.callLevel.format: 'If the closing level of the Index on any Review Date (other than the first and final Review Dates) is greater than or equal to the Initial Value' — i.e. call level = 100% of Initial Value unless stated otherwise
autocall.callLevel._note: JPMorgan autocall is typically at 100% of Initial Value (par call). The call level is NOT given as a named 'Call Level' field but is implied as 'the Initial Value'.

autocall.callSchedule.format: List of dates with '(final Review Date)' marking the last one. Often quarterly or monthly. Call excludes first and final Review Dates.

coupon.paymentDates.format: List of dates, typically quarterly, last one is the Maturity Date

autocall.callSettlementDate.format: 'the first Interest Payment Date immediately following that Review Date'

parties.issuer.name.format: 'JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC, a direct, wholly owned finance subsidiary of JPMorgan Chase & Co.'

JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC.general_notes: JPMorgan 424B2 pricing supplements (jpm_424b2-XXXXX.htm) use a highly structured two-column 'Key Terms' table near page top, with brown/gold section headers. Index is singular (not multi-asset). Autocall trigger = Initial Value (100%). Interest Barrier and Trigger Value are often the same percentage and labeled together as 'Interest Barrier/Trigger Value'. Coupon is quarterly with a per-$1,000 dollar amount stated alongside the rate. Review Dates serve dual purpose as both coupon observation dates and autocall observation dates. Memory coupon feature present in some filings (coupon accumulates across missed periods).

Bank of Montreal.key_terms_position: early — 'Terms of the Notes' table appears within first 100 lines (cover page), followed by 'Specific Terms of the Notes' table, then 'Key Terms of the Notes' table on page 2
Bank of Montreal.document_structure: Three-section cover: (1) 'Terms of the Notes' — high-level dates/CUSIP/notional table; (2) 'Specific Terms of the Notes' — multi-column term sheet with Reference Assets, tickers, Initial Levels, rates, Coupon Barrier Level, Trigger Level; (3) 'Key Terms of the Notes' — narrative table on page 2 with definitions

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.format: Full name with ticker in quotes: 'The Russell 2000® Index (ticker symbol "RTY")'. Multiple assets listed in same cell.
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.typical_location: Specific Terms table header row; Key Terms table first row

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].tick.label_in_doc: Ticker Symbol column in Specific Terms table
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].tick.format: Exchange ticker, e.g. 'RTY', 'SPX'. Also stated in parentheses: '(ticker symbol "RTY")'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].tick.typical_location: Specific Terms of the Notes table, 'Ticker Symbol' column

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.label_in_doc: Initial Level column; Initial Level: in Key Terms table
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.typical_location: Specific Terms table 'Initial Level' column; Key Terms table 'Initial Level' row

coupon.rate.format: '0.7083% per month (approximately 8.50% per annum)'. Dollar amount: '$7.083 for each $1,000 in principal amount'
coupon.rate.typical_location: Specific Terms table 'Contingent Interest Rate' column; Key Terms table 'Contingent Interest Rate' row

coupon.barrierLevel.label_in_doc: Coupon Barrier Level* (column header); Coupon Barrier Level: (Key Terms row)
coupon.barrierLevel.format: Absolute level with percentage: '1,949.187, 80.00% of its Initial Level'. Extract 0.80.
coupon.barrierLevel.typical_location: Specific Terms table 'Coupon Barrier Level' column; Key Terms table

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.label_in_doc: Trigger Level* (column header); Trigger Level: (Key Terms row)
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.format: Absolute level with percentage: '1,949.187, 80.00% of its Initial Level'. In this issuer's filings, Trigger Level and Coupon Barrier Level are often the same value.
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.typical_location: Specific Terms table 'Trigger Level' column; Key Terms table

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerEffect.format: 'A Trigger Event will be deemed to occur if the Final Level of any Reference Asset is less than its Trigger Level on the Valuation Date.' Maps to 'default' in PRISM.

coupon.observationDates.format: 'Three trading days prior to each scheduled Contingent Coupon Payment Date'

coupon.paymentDates.format: 'Interest, if payable, will be paid on the 3rd day of each month (or, if such day is not a business day, the next following business day), beginning on [date] and ending on the Maturity Date.'

structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.format: Date string in Terms of the Notes table
structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, first row

structuredProductsGeneric.issueDate.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table and Key Terms table

structuredProductsGeneric.maturityDate.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table and Key Terms table

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table and Key Terms table

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.format: 'With respect to each Reference Asset, the closing level of that Reference Asset on the Valuation Date.'

structuredProductsGeneric.notional.label_in_doc: Principal Amount column in Specific Terms table

Bank of Montreal.general_notes: Bank of Montreal 424B2 supplements use three layered tables: 'Terms of the Notes' (brief dates), 'Specific Terms of the Notes' (multi-column grid), then 'Key Terms of the Notes' (narrative). Multiple Reference Assets are common (worst-of structure on RTY and SPX). Coupon Barrier Level and Trigger Level are often equal in barrier coupon products. Ticker symbols used are exchange tickers (RTY, SPX), not Bloomberg tickers. Calculation agent is BMOCM (BMO Capital Markets Corp.). Issuer entity name: Bank of Montreal (CIK 927971). Note the 'Contingent Coupons' heading in Key Terms describes contingent payment mechanics.

UBS AG.key_terms_position: early — product summary and key economic terms appear on cover page within first 300 lines. A structured table ('Note Offering' section) shows all key parameters in a multi-column grid.
UBS AG.document_structure: UBS uses a distinctive two-column front page layout: left column has 'Investment Description' narrative + 'Features' bullet points; right column has 'Key Dates' table. Below is 'Note Offering' table with per-underlying rows showing: Underlying Assets, Bloomberg Tickers, Contingent Coupon Rate, Initial Levels, Call Threshold Levels, Coupon Barriers, Downside Thresholds, Buffer, CUSIP, ISIN.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.label_in_doc: Underlying Assets column in Note Offering table
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.format: Full index name, e.g. 'Russell 2000® Index', 'S&P 500® Index'

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].bloo.format: Bloomberg ticker, e.g. 'RTY', 'SPX'. Column header explicitly says 'Bloomberg Tickers'.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.label_in_doc: Initial Levels column; also 'Initial Level' in payoff section
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.format: Absolute index level shown in Note Offering table. In hypothetical examples: '2,500.00 (which is equal to 100.00% of the Initial Level)'

coupon.rate.label_in_doc: Contingent Coupon Rate column in Note Offering table; also 'Contingent Coupon Rate:' in Preliminary Terms
coupon.rate.format: Percentage per annum, e.g. '7.15% per annum'. Monthly equivalent may be shown in Preliminary Terms section: '6.00% per annum (or 0.50% per month)'
coupon.rate.typical_location: Note Offering table; Preliminary Terms section

autocall.callLevel.label_in_doc: Call Threshold Levels column in Note Offering table
autocall.callLevel.format: Percentage of Initial Level: '100.00% of its Initial Level'
autocall.callLevel.typical_location: Note Offering table, 'Call Threshold Levels' column

coupon.barrierLevel.label_in_doc: Coupon Barriers column in Note Offering table; 'Coupon Barrier:' in Preliminary Terms
coupon.barrierLevel.format: Percentage of Initial Level: '80.00% of its Initial Level'. Absolute values also shown: '2,000.00 (which is equal to 80.00% of the Initial Level)'

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.label_in_doc: Downside Thresholds column in Note Offering table
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.format: Percentage of Initial Level: '85.00% of its Initial Level'. Note: UBS uses 'Downside Threshold' for barrier (principal loss trigger), which may differ from Coupon Barrier.

barrier.buffer.label_in_doc: Buffer column in Note Offering table; 'buffer' in narrative
barrier.buffer.format: Percentage, e.g. '15.00%'. This means losses below Downside Threshold are absorbed up to Buffer amount before principal is affected.
barrier.buffer._note: UBS Buffer Autocallable product has a buffer feature: principal loss = (decline beyond buffer). If buffer = 15%, downside threshold = 85%, loss only occurs below 85% and is multiplied by 1/(1-buffer). Verify against PRISM schema buffer field.

structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.label_in_doc: Trade Date** in Key Dates table
structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.typical_location: Key Dates table on front cover

structuredProductsGeneric.issueDate.label_in_doc: Settlement Date** in Key Dates table
structuredProductsGeneric.issueDate.typical_location: Key Dates table on front cover

structuredProductsGeneric.maturityDate.label_in_doc: Maturity Date in Key Dates table

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.label_in_doc: Final Valuation Date in Key Dates table

autocall.callSchedule.label_in_doc: Observation Dates in Key Dates table
autocall.callSchedule.typical_location: Key Dates table; detailed schedule on later page

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.label_in_doc: Final Level in payoff formula tables
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.format: Absolute level on Final Valuation Date

UBS AG.general_notes: UBS 424B2 supplements (ubs_424b2-XXXXX.htm) have a distinctive two-panel front page with olive/green section headers (#788D41 background). The 'Note Offering' table is the primary structured data source and contains all key economic parameters per underlying asset. UBS uses 'Downside Threshold' as the principal loss barrier (not 'Trigger Level') and has a separate 'Buffer' parameter. 'Call Threshold Levels' is the autocall trigger. This is a 'Buffer Autocallable Contingent Yield Notes' product structure. Initial Levels are shown as bullet points in the Note Offering table. UBS uses Bloomberg tickers explicitly labeled 'Bloomberg Tickers' (not 'Ticker Symbol'). The 'Contingent Coupon Rate' applies to all underlying assets collectively (one rate for the worst-of basket).

GS Finance Corp. / Goldman Sachs Group I.key_terms_position: early — product description on cover page (page 1); 'Key Terms and Assumptions' box appears around line 870 in the body, on approximately page 10 of the supplement
GS Finance Corp. / Goldman Sachs Group I.document_structure: Goldman Sachs supplements begin with a dense cover page narrative describing coupon mechanics and maturity payoff. A 'Key Terms and Assumptions' bordered box appears in the examples section. Formal term definitions appear in a 'Terms of Your Notes' section later in the document.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.label_in_doc: Not a table field — embedded in product title and cover narrative. 'underlier' used throughout body text.
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.format: Full index name in product title, e.g. 'S&P 500® Futures 40% VT Adaptive Response 6% Decrement Index (USD) ER'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.typical_location: Product title on cover; 'The Underlier' section heading

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.label_in_doc: Initial underlier level (set on the trade date):
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.format: 'the closing level of the underlier on the trade date'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.typical_location: Terms of Your Notes section; Key Terms and Assumptions table

coupon.rate.label_in_doc: Coupon in Key Terms and Assumptions table
coupon.rate.format: Memory coupon formula: '(i) the product of $11.459 (1.1459% monthly, or the potential for up to approximately 13.75% per annum) times the number of observation dates that have occurred ... minus (ii) the sum of all coupons previously paid'. Extract annual rate = 13.75%.
coupon.rate.typical_location: Key Terms and Assumptions box; cover narrative
coupon.rate._note: Goldman Sachs frequently uses a MEMORY COUPON structure where coupon = (cumulative rate × count of observation dates) minus previously paid. This is NOT a simple periodic coupon — it catches up missed coupons. Total potential annual rate = 13.75% in this example.

coupon.barrierLevel.label_in_doc: Coupon trigger level in Key Terms and Assumptions table
coupon.barrierLevel.format: Percentage of initial underlier level: '70% of the initial underlier level'

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.typical_location: Key Terms and Assumptions box; Terms of Your Notes

autocall.callLevel.label_in_doc: No explicit 'Call Level' label — stated in autocall condition: 'greater than or equal to the initial underlier level'
autocall.callLevel.format: Autocall trigger = initial underlier level (100%). Stated in narrative: 'automatically called if the closing level of the index on any such observation date is greater than or equal to the initial underlier level'
autocall.callLevel.typical_location: Cover page narrative; Terms of Your Notes section

autocall.callSchedule.label_in_doc: Call observation dates (set on the trade date):
autocall.callSchedule.format: Date range: 'expected to be each coupon observation date commencing in March 2026 and ending in August 2030'

coupon.observationDates.label_in_doc: Coupon observation dates (set on the trade date):
coupon.observationDates.format: 'expected to be the 30th day of each month, commencing in October 2025 and ending in September 2030'

coupon.paymentDates.label_in_doc: Coupon payment dates (set on the trade date):
coupon.paymentDates.format: 'expected to be the third business day after each coupon observation date'

structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.label_in_doc: Original issue date (set on the trade date):
structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.format: Trade date is when terms are set. 'expected to be September 30, 2025'
structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.typical_location: Cover page; Terms of Your Notes

structuredProductsGeneric.maturityDate.label_in_doc: Stated maturity date (set on the trade date):

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.label_in_doc: Determination date (set on the trade date):
structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.format: 'the last coupon observation date, expected to be September 30, 2030'

GS Finance Corp. / Goldman Sachs Group I.general_notes: Goldman Sachs (GS Finance Corp.) supplements use dense cover-page narrative text rather than a term table. Key terminology differs significantly: 'underlier' instead of 'underlying'/'reference asset'; 'coupon trigger level' for coupon barrier; 'trigger buffer level' for principal barrier. GS uses a MEMORY COUPON structure where each coupon payment equals accumulated coupon amount minus previously paid amounts — this means a coupon can be very large after multiple missed periods. 'Call observation dates' are a subset of 'coupon observation dates'. The 'determination date' is the final observation date (equivalent to valuation date). Goldman uses 'trade date' consistently while other issuers use 'pricing date'. GS Finance Corp. is the issuer; Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is the guarantor.

section_headings: Observation Dates, Contingent Payment Dates and Call Payment Dates
Contingent Coupon Payment and Redemption Amount Determination
BofA Finance LLC.key_terms_position: early — bullet-point summary on cover page within first 200 lines; structured 'Terms of the Notes' table with blue (#0080FF) row headers appears around line 600
BofA Finance LLC.document_structure: BofA supplements begin with a bullet-point feature summary, followed by a detailed 'Terms of the Notes' table using alternating grey/white rows with blue field-label cells. A separate table shows all observation dates and payment dates. A 'Contingent Coupon Payment and Redemption Amount Determination' section has payoff diagrams.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.format: 'The Nasdaq-100® Index (Bloomberg symbol: "NDX"), the Russell 2000® Index (Bloomberg symbol: "RTY") and the S&P 500® Index (Bloomberg symbol: "SPX"), each a price return index.'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Underlyings' row

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].bloo.label_in_doc: Bloomberg symbol in parentheses after index name
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].bloo.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Underlyings' row

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.label_in_doc: Starting Value: (blue cell in Terms table)
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Starting Value' row

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.label_in_doc: Ending Value: (blue cell in Terms table)
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.format: 'With respect to each Underlying, its Observation Value on the Valuation Date.'
underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Ending Value' row

coupon.rate.format: '$7.50 per $1,000.00 in principal amount of Notes (equal to a rate of 0.75% per month or 9.00% per annum)'
coupon.rate.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table; cover bullets

coupon.barrierLevel.label_in_doc: Coupon Barrier: (blue cell in Terms table)
coupon.barrierLevel.format: Per-underlying values: 'NDX: 17,275.99, which is 70.00% of its Starting Value'. Extract 0.70.
coupon.barrierLevel.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Coupon Barrier' row

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.label_in_doc: Threshold Value: (blue cell in Terms table)
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.format: Per-underlying values: 'NDX: 17,275.99, which is 70.00% of its Starting Value'. In this product, Threshold Value = Coupon Barrier.
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Threshold Value' row

coupon.observationDates.label_in_doc: Observation Dates: (blue cell in Terms table)
coupon.observationDates.format: Monthly dates. 'subject to postponement as described under ...'
coupon.observationDates.typical_location: Terms of the Notes table, 'Observation Dates' row; separate 'Observation Dates, Contingent Payment Dates and Call Payment Dates' table

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.format: Date string. Note: 'August 31, 2026, subject to postponement'

autocall.callSchedule.label_in_doc: Cover bullet: 'Beginning on January 5, 2026, callable monthly at our option'
autocall.callSchedule.format: Issuer-option (not automatic) call. Call dates = Observation Dates starting from first callable date.
autocall.callSchedule.typical_location: Cover page bullet summary; Observation Dates table
autocall.callSchedule._note: BofA callable notes use ISSUER CALL OPTION (not automatic/mandatory autocall). The issuer may call at its option, not based on index level. This differs from JPMorgan/UBS autocall structure.

underlyingTerms.barrierObservation.format: Monthly observation (European-style at each monthly observation date, not daily/continuous)

BofA Finance LLC.general_notes: BofA Finance LLC (BofA Finance or 'Issuer', guaranteed by Bank of America Corporation 'BAC') uses blue cell headers (#0080FF) in their 'Terms of the Notes' table. Key differences: 'Starting Value' instead of 'Initial Level'; 'Ending Value' instead of 'Final Level'; 'Threshold Value' for the principal barrier; 'Coupon Barrier' for the coupon observation trigger. This is an ISSUER-CALLABLE (not autocall) product — the issuer decides whether to call, not an automatic trigger. Multiple underlyings are worst-of structure. 'Observation Value' is the closing level on each Observation Date.

field_level_hints._description: Cross-issuer field-level extraction hints, applied regardless of issuer. These are grounded in actual terminology observed across all filings.

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.description: The barrier level expressed as a decimal (e.g. 0.70 for 70%). This is the downside protection threshold — if the underlying falls below this level on/before the relevant observation date, principal is at risk.
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.value_format: Convert: '70%' → 0.70; '70% of the Initial Level' → 0.70; absolute level like '5,350.77, 80.00% of its Initial Level' → 0.80
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel.caution: Do NOT confuse with the coupon barrier (which triggers coupon non-payment) or the autocall trigger (which triggers early redemption). The barrier trigger level is specifically about PRINCIPAL LOSS at maturity.

barrier.triggerDetails.triggerEffect.description: What happens when barrier is breached. In these structured notes, breach = investor loses principal proportional to index decline beyond barrier.
barrier.triggerDetails.triggerEffect.value_format: Map to PRISM enum. Typically 'default' for standard barrier notes where breach causes downside loss.

barrier.triggerDetails.observationType.description: How/when the barrier is observed — European (at maturity only), American (continuous/daily), or periodic (at scheduled dates).

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Trigger is observed at final Review Date only — European style
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Trigger Level assessed on Valuation Date (European style)
issuer_specific.UBS AG: Downside Threshold assessed at Final Valuation Date (European style)
issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: trigger buffer level assessed at determination date (European style)
issuer_specific.BofA Finance: Threshold Value assessed at Valuation Date (European style)

barrier.triggerDetails.observationType.value_format: Most 424B2 barrier notes in this corpus use European observation (assessed at maturity/valuation date only). Daily barrier monitoring is less common but would be stated explicitly.

coupon.rate.description: The contingent or fixed coupon rate as a decimal per annum.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Contingent Interest Rate (e.g. '13.75% per annum, payable at a rate of 3.4375% per quarter')
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Contingent Interest Rate (e.g. '0.7083% per month (approximately 8.50% per annum)')
issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: Coupon stated as memory formula: '$11.459 per observation date' = 1.1459% monthly = 13.75% per annum
issuer_specific.BofA Finance: Contingent coupon rate of 9.00% per annum (0.75% per month)

coupon.rate.value_format: Convert to annual decimal: '8.50% per annum' → 0.085; '0.7083% per month' → annualize: 0.007083 × 12 = 0.085; '13.75% per annum' → 0.1375
coupon.rate.caution: Goldman Sachs uses a memory coupon structure where the per-period amount is stated as a dollar figure ($11.459 per $1,000) × cumulative count minus previous payments. Extract the per-annum equivalent rate.

coupon.barrierLevel.description: The level below which the coupon is NOT paid on an observation date. Expressed as a decimal (e.g. 0.70 for 70%).

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Interest Barrier (same value as Trigger Value in most filings)
issuer_specific.UBS AG: Coupon Barriers (column header); Coupon Barrier: (in Preliminary Terms)

coupon.barrierLevel.value_format: Convert percentage to decimal: '80.00% of its Initial Level' → 0.80; absolute value '1,949.187, 80.00% of its Initial Level' → 0.80
coupon.barrierLevel.caution: Do NOT confuse with the barrier trigger level (principal loss threshold). The coupon barrier controls income payments; the trigger level/downside threshold controls principal repayment at maturity.

coupon.frequency.description: How often the contingent coupon is observed and paid.
coupon.frequency.value_format: Map to PRISM enum: 'monthly' (12 per year), 'quarterly' (4 per year), 'semi-annual' (2), 'annual' (1)

issuer_patterns.JPMorgan: Quarterly (quarterly Review Dates → quarterly Interest Payment Dates)
issuer_patterns.Goldman Sachs: Monthly (monthly observation dates, third business day after)

coupon.paymentDates.description: Array of dates on which contingent coupons are paid if the coupon condition is met.

coupon.observationDates.description: Dates on which the underlying is observed to determine coupon eligibility.
common_synonyms: Contingent Coupon Payment Dates observation component

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Review Dates (serve as both coupon observation and autocall observation)
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Observation Dates (three trading days before each Contingent Coupon Payment Date)

coupon.observationDates.value_format: Array of ISO date strings. Note: some issuers (BofA) use the same date for both coupon observation and call evaluation.

autocall.callLevel.description: The index level (as fraction of initial) that triggers an automatic call/redemption. Usually 100% of initial level.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Not labeled 'Call Level' — autocall triggers when closing level >= Initial Value. Implied call level = 1.00.
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: No autocall in barrier coupon products (non-callable); autocall only in autocallable variants
issuer_specific.UBS AG: Call Threshold Levels column: '100.00% of its Initial Level' → 1.00
issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: Not labeled — stated in narrative as '≥ initial underlier level'
issuer_specific.BofA Finance: Issuer-option call (not level-triggered autocall) — callable at issuer's discretion

autocall.callLevel.value_format: Decimal: '100% of Initial Value' → 1.00; '105% of Initial Level' → 1.05

autocall.callSchedule.description: The dates on which the autocall condition is evaluated.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Review Dates excluding first and final: 'other than the first and final Review Dates'
issuer_specific.UBS AG: Observation Dates beginning after 12 months: 'Monthly (callable after 12 months)'
issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: Call observation dates starting March 2026 through August 2030 (subset of coupon observation dates)

autocall.callSchedule.value_format: Array of {date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', level: 0.XX} objects, or array of ISO dates if call level is uniform

autocall.callFrequency.description: How frequently the autocall condition is evaluated.

autocall.memory.description: Whether the autocall structure has a memory coupon feature (cumulative coupon catch-up).
autocall.memory.value_format: Boolean: true if coupon formula includes minus previously-paid amounts
autocall.memory._note: Goldman Sachs filings explicitly use memory coupon: 'times the number of observation dates that have occurred ... minus the sum of all coupons previously paid'. Look for this formula structure to identify memory feature.

structuredProductsGeneric.tradeDate.description: The date on which the economic terms are set (pricing/trade date).

issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: trade date (set on the trade date)

structuredProductsGeneric.issueDate.description: The settlement/issue date when notes are delivered.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Original Issue Date (Settlement Date): 'On or about October 3, 2025'

structuredProductsGeneric.issueDate.value_format: ISO date string YYYY-MM-DD. Often T+2 or T+3 from trade/pricing date.

structuredProductsGeneric.maturityDate.description: The final date on which notes mature and principal/final payment is made.
structuredProductsGeneric.maturityDate.value_format: ISO date string YYYY-MM-DD. Typically a few days after the final valuation/determination date.

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.description: The final observation date used to determine maturity payoff.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Final Review Date (the last in the Review Dates list, marked '(final Review Date)')

structuredProductsGeneric.valuationDate.value_format: ISO date string YYYY-MM-DD. Typically 2-5 business days before maturity date.

structuredProductsGeneric.currency._note: All filings in this corpus are USD-denominated. Bank of Montreal notes are stated in USD not CAD.

structuredProductsGeneric.notional.description: Total principal amount / face amount of the note offering.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].name.description: Full name of each reference asset/underlying index or security.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Index (singular; one underlying per note typically)

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].bloo.description: Bloomberg ticker symbol for the underlying index or security.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Bloomberg ticker in parentheses: '(Bloomberg ticker: MQUSSVA)'
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Ticker Symbol column (exchange ticker, e.g. RTY, SPX — NOT Bloomberg format)
issuer_specific._note: Bank of Montreal uses exchange tickers (RTY, SPX) not full Bloomberg tickers. Other issuers use Bloomberg format.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.description: The starting reference level of each underlying asset, typically the closing level on the pricing/trade date.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Initial Value: 'The closing level of the Index on the Pricing Date, which was 747.51'
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Initial Level column in Specific Terms table (e.g. '2,436.484')
issuer_specific.UBS AG: Initial Levels column (placeholder bullet '•' on cover; actual value set on Trade Date)
issuer_specific.Goldman Sachs: initial underlier level (set on the trade date)

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].init.value_format: Absolute index level as number (no conversion). For multi-underlying notes, values are given per underlying.

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.description: The closing level of each underlying on the final valuation date, used to compute maturity payoff.

issuer_specific.JPMorgan: Final Value: 'The closing level of the Index on the final Review Date'
issuer_specific.Bank of Montreal: Final Level: 'the closing level of that Reference Asset on the Valuation Date'

underlyingTerms.underlyingAssets[*].fina.value_format: Not known until maturity. If present in document, it is a hypothetical example value.

settlement.type.description: Whether settlement is cash or physical delivery.
settlement.type.value_format: Map to PRISM enum. All filings in this corpus are cash-settled.
settlement.type._note: All observed 424B2 notes settle in cash. Physical delivery would require explicit statement.

settlement.settlementDate.description: The date on which cash payments are made at maturity.
settlement.settlementDate.value_format: ISO date string. For these notes, settlement date at maturity = maturity date.

parties.issuer.name.description: Legal entity that issues the notes.

known_issuers.JPMorgan: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC (wholly owned subsidiary of JPMorgan Chase & Co.)
known_issuers.Goldman Sachs: GS Finance Corp. (guaranteed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.)
known_issuers.BofA: BofA Finance LLC (guaranteed by Bank of America Corporation)

parties.guarantor.name.description: Legal entity guaranteeing the notes, if any.

parties.calculationAgent.name.description: Entity responsible for calculating payments and making determinations.

known_agents.UBS: UBS AG or UBS Securities LLC

identifiers.isin.value_format: 12-character alphanumeric string starting with country code, e.g. 'US90310EGG44'
identifiers.isin._note: UBS filings include ISIN in the Note Offering table. Other issuers may not.

extraction_strategy._description: Recommended approach for extracting PRISM fields from 424B2 supplements
general_order: 1. Identify issuer from document header/cover page
2. Locate the primary terms table (issuer-specific section heading)
3. Extract all key date fields first (tradeDate, issueDate, maturityDate, valuationDate)
4. Extract underlying assets and their initial levels
5. Extract coupon rate and coupon barrier level
6. Extract principal barrier/trigger level (may be same as coupon barrier or different)
common_traps: JPMorgan uses 'Interest Barrier/Trigger Value' as a COMBINED field — extract both coupon.barrierLevel and barrier.triggerDetails.triggerLevel from the same value
Goldman Sachs uses 'underlier' not 'underlying' — do not miss field matches due to terminology
Bank of Montreal 'Terms of the Notes' table is a summary — the 'Key Terms of the Notes' table on page 2 has full definitions
UBS 'Initial Levels' column shows bullet points (•) on preliminary supplements — actual values are set on Trade Date
BofA 'callable at our option' is ISSUER-OPTION call, not an autocall based on index level
Goldman Sachs memory coupon: per-period amount = (monthly_rate × cumulative_count) − sum_previously_paid. Do not treat single-period amount as the coupon rate.
Percentage values may be given as both percentage string ('80.00%') and absolute level ('1,949.187') — prefer percentage for PRISM triggerLevel fields
Valuation Date ≠ Maturity Date — maturity is typically 2-5 business days after final observation/valuation
